name: Blue Mitchell
abstract: Richard Allen "Blue" Mitchell (March 13, 1930 – May 21, 1979) was an American trumpeter and composer who worked in jazz, rhythm and blues, soul, rock, and funk. He recorded albums as leader and sideman for Riverside, Mainstream Records, and Blue Note.

concerts.label: John Mayall, Delbert & Glen, and Tranquility

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to a lot of Blue Note records, Lee Morgan and Hank Mobley and Horace Silver, Junior Cook and Blue Mitchell. No trombone on that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: I ended up in that piano chair, and did some recordings, and played gloriously with that band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: He worked with me a lot after I had him in a small band with Art Pepper and Cat Anderson... We worked in San Francisco around up there, and it was a real good little band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: So, so we all--five of us, man--but actually it was six of us--me; Mitchell Robinson... Blue Mitchell; Johnny Burdine... and a trombone player named Abe Meeks

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Clifford Brown, Blue Mitchell, Kenny Dorham... The first one is with Horace Silver and Art Blakey and Blue Mitchell, and Gene Ramey.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Young Snooky
about_quotes.evidence: I played in his band, man... Blue is Blue Mitchell... we enjoyed playing with one another.
about_quotes.doc_title: Young Snooky — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
